title: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae
what_description: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae is a subspecies of the Hieracium lachenalii species within the Hieracium genus. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiea is characterized by its biennial to perennial habit, and has a rosette of leaves with elliptical to lanceolate leaflets. The plant produces yellow to orange flowers in a capitulum, surrounded by a whorl of phyllaries, and its inflorescence is a corymb. The flowers are hermaphroditic and lack a distinct corolla, instead having a ring of florets surrounding a central disc.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae are inflammation, which it reduces by its anti-inflammatory properties, and rheumatism, due to its ability to relieve pain and stiffness. Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae also treats fever by reducing its intensity and duration, and digestive issues, such as gastroenteritis, by preventing the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Additionally, it is effective in treating skin irritation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating skin conditions like eczema. Furthermore, Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae has been shown to combat fungal infections, bacterial infections, and diarrhea, as well as infections in general, and is used as a complementary treatment for cancer, while also providing relief from respiratory issues and allergies, and migraines.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae are associated with its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant also exhibits antimicrobial properties due to its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, likely as a result of the bioactive compounds saponins and volatile oils. The anti-inflammatory activity of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae is thought to be related to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Its analgesic properties are attributed to the bioactive compounds glycosides and sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce pain perception. Furthermore, the plant's antibacterial properties are due to the presence of phenolic compounds and other bioactive compounds, which may help combat bacterial infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae are flavonoids, which include quercetin and kaempferol, that exhibit antioxidant properties and anti-inflammatory effects, flavonoids have been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, the plant contains phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Tannins, including gallotannins and ellagitannins, are also present in Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae and have been reported to display antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Triterpenoids, particularly oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, have been isolated from the plant and have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Furthermore, coumarins, including scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside, have been identified in Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae and have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae are primarily focused on its bitter compounds, which have been traditionally used for digestive and liver issues. An infusion is typically made by steeping the dried flowers in boiling water to create a bitter tea, which is used to aid digestion and relieve liver complaints. A tincture is also prepared by steeping the flowers in a solvent, such as ethanol, to create a concentrated extract that is used in homeopathy. To treat skin issues, a salve can be made by infusing the plant in a carrier oil, such as olive oil, to create a topical cream that is applied directly to the affected area. An ointment can also be prepared by mixing the infused oil with other ingredients, such as beeswax, to create a thicker, more protective topical application.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae improperly include experience nausea and cause diarrhea due to its potential toxicity, inducing allergic reaction in individuals who are sensitive to its compounds, increasing blood pressure in people with pre-existing hypertension, and inducing nausea due to its emetic properties. This is because Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae contains sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds that can be toxic to humans if ingested in large quantities or for extended periods. The use of this plant for medicinal purposes is not well documented and its safety profile is largely unknown. Improper use can lead to serious health consequences, including gastrointestinal disturbances, cardiovascular issues, and allergic reactions. It is essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional before using Hieracium lachenalii subsp. lortetiae, especially for individuals with pre-existing medical conditions.
